name: Kimbundu (Mbundu)
notes: STRUCTURAL ANCESTRY / CONTACT NODE (metadata only, not a modelled G2P target). Kimbundu is referenced only as a representative West-Central African (Bantu) adstrate on Brazilian Portuguese, standing in for the Kimbundu/Kikongo/Yoruba lexical layer brought by the enslaved population (samba, moleque, caçula, cafuné, quilombo). No phonology is claimed; the minimal grapheme set is a placeholder (afa.json / trk.json stub pattern). Glottolog namb1293.

sources.author: Castro, Y. P. de
sources.title: Falares africanos na Bahia: um vocabulário afro-brasileiro
sources.notes: Reference for the West-Central African (Kimbundu/Kikongo/Yoruba) lexical contribution to Brazilian Portuguese (structural node only).
